message:
fix(db/sync): tolerate orphaned recipients in sync_message_recipients_json

Mirrors the mail_explorer orphaned-row fix into the materialised
`recipients_json` column that the message ingestion path writes back
on every message. Previously the SELECT used an inner
`JOIN agents a ON a.id = mr.agent_id`, so if the agent row was
deleted out from under a pending message, the recipient disappeared
from the cached JSON blob entirely — the next reader saw a message
that claimed to have no recipients.

SQL changes
-----------
* `JOIN agents a ON a.id = mr.agent_id` →
  `LEFT JOIN agents a ON a.id = mr.agent_id` so rows with a missing
  agent still surface.
* `a.name AS name` →
  `COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.name), ''), '[unknown-agent-' || mr.agent_id || ']') AS name`.
  Missing, empty, or whitespace-only names are replaced with a
  stable `[unknown-agent-<id>]` sentinel so downstream readers can
  both see the row exists and identify which agent id lost its
  metadata.
* ORDER BY clause updated to sort by the same coalesced expression
  instead of `a.name COLLATE NOCASE`, so orphans sort consistently
  with live rows and the ordering is deterministic.

Test coverage (`tests::sync_message_recipients_json_keeps_orphaned_recipient_rows_visible`)
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
New unit test that:

  1. Sets up a project, a sender, and a recipient agent.
  2. Dispatches a root message addressed to `Recipient1`.
  3. Deletes the `Recipient1` row out from the `agents` table.
  4. Runs `sync_message_recipients_json(&conn, msg_id)` to simulate
     a post-delete sync.
  5. Reads back `messages.recipients_json` and asserts it contains
     the expected `[unknown-agent-<id>]` placeholder instead of
     either losing the recipient silently or rendering an empty
     string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
